about.title: About me
about.body: I’m a product designer with 6 years of experience, including 4 years in product teams and 1 year in a team lead role. I work across UX and UI — from research and user interaction to prototyping, building design systems, and collaborating with stakeholders.

process.title: My process
process.description: From understanding the problem to validating the solution, each step is grounded in clarity and collaboration
process.step1Body: I start with the problem, business context, and user needs
process.step2Body: I map user flows, identify friction points, and shape clear interaction logic
process.step3Body: I turn ideas into wireframes, interfaces, and scalable UI solutions
process.step4Body: I validate design hypotheses through usability testing and iterate on solutions based on user feedback to improve overall user experience
process.step5Body: I work closely with product, development, and stakeholders to keep decisions aligned

expertise.title: Expertise
expertise.description: I work across UX, UI, and product thinking — from problem analysis to interface solutions and design quality

experience.title: Experience
experience.description: A journey from UX/UI design to product roles, leadership, and launching independent initiatives
experience.rizzultBullet1: Led product design across key B2B workflows, from campaign setup to management and reporting
experience.rizzultBullet2: Built a scalable design system that became the foundation for multiple products
experience.rizzultBullet3: Translated a complex influencer marketing service model into a clear and usable product experience
experience.acmeBullet1: Structured complex operational workflows into clear tools for campaign and process management
experience.acmeBullet2: Led the design of an internal admin panel that supported the company’s day-to-day operations
experience.freelanceBullet1: Designed a workforce management platform initially built for a specific business and later shaped for subscription-based scaling
experience.freelanceBullet2: Created core workflows for business setup, location and staff management, scheduling, and payroll tracking
experience.freelanceBullet3: Helped turn a custom business tool into a scalable product foundation with SaaS potential
experience.brandlabBullet1: Optimized the user journey for property purchases, boosting conversion by up to 30%
experience.brandlabBullet2: Designed a mobile app for owner-listed property rentals
experience.uniBullet1: Customized the product to meet specific client needs

footerCta.title: Let's connect
footerCta.description: Open to product design roles, collaborations, and meaningful conversations.

case.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA

influencers.title: Rizzult — App for Influencers
influencers.overview: Transformed Rizzult's influencer workflow from a manual, scout-led process into a mobile self-service platform, allowing creators to independently discover projects, apply to campaigns, and manage collaborations.
influencers.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
influencers.flow2: Review the company and campaign rules
influencers.impact1Body: Enabled influencers to manage key collaboration steps independently in one mobile experience.
influencers.impact2Body: Improved access to brand information, campaign requirements, and participation guidelines before applying.
influencers.impact3Body: Structured the flow from application to content approval and publication confirmation into a clearer process.
influencers.goalBody: The main challenge was to design the product from the ground up and turn a broad idea into a clear, usable mobile experience for influencers. The app needed to combine two different value areas — creator growth and monetization — in a way that felt simple, relevant, and easy to navigate on mobile.
influencers.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
influencers.role1Body: Mapping complex core product logic and multi-sided marketplace flows.
influencers.role2Body: Establishing a high-fidelity visual design language for the mobile-first era.
influencers.role3Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
influencers.role4Body: Developing complex interactive prototypes for rapid user validation.
influencers.processIntro: Working closely with the Product Manager, Product Owner, and creators to shape, test, and improve the product experience.
influencers.process1Body: Collaborative work with the Product Director to shape app structure and core logic.
influencers.process2Body: Translating ideas into interface concepts, flows, and interactive prototypes.
influencers.process3Body: Reviewing and validating the experience with a small group of UGC creators.
influencers.process4Body: Iterated on product flows and interactions based on testing in Figma and the live product.
influencers.decisionsIntro: Key UX decisions were focused on reducing friction, improving clarity, and supporting creators throughout the flow.
influencers.decision1Body: Many creators were not used to handling the full campaign flow themselves, since similar processes had previously been supported by scouts. I focused on making the experience as simple and guided as possible.
influencers.decision2Title: Making relevant opportunities easy to find
influencers.decision2Body: A key decision was to help influencers quickly identify projects that matched their profile and were worth applying to, reducing the effort required to browse and evaluate options.
influencers.decision3Title: Simplifying the application and submission flow
influencers.decision3Body: The participation flow was designed to feel lightweight and clear, so users could easily apply to a project, complete the required steps, and later submit content for moderation without unnecessary friction.
influencers.decision4Body: Special attention was given to statuses, progress states, and next-step clarity. Users needed to always understand where they were in the process — whether waiting for approval, preparing content, submitting materials, or expecting payment.
influencers.decision5Body: Since the product was mobile-first, I designed the interface to support quick scanning, clear prioritization, and fast actions, especially for repeat tasks.
influencers.impactIntro: Early outcomes from launch showed the product's potential to support creators more independently and efficiently.
influencers.impactItem1: The product was launched and showed early signs that creators could independently find projects, apply to campaigns, and complete publishing-related actions.
influencers.impactItem2: The experience helped validate a more self-service model, with the potential to reduce reliance on scouts and lower manual operational effort.
influencers.impactItem3: Positive feedback from influencers highlighted the value of the app's educational and utility-driven content.
influencers.impactItem4: Features related to content planning helped creators organize their work more effectively and increased the app's practical day-to-day relevance.
influencers.impactItem5: These elements supported stronger user loyalty and encouraged more regular engagement with the product.

manager.title: Rizzult Talent Manager
manager.overview: Rizzult Talent Manager is a mobile scouting tool built to help recruit influencers into Rizzult. By turning everyday users into scouts and guiding them through a simple outreach flow, the product made influencer acquisition more scalable and less operationally complex.
manager.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
manager.flow2: Repost the story to your page
manager.flow3: Leave a comment on the post
manager.impact1Body: Enabled everyday users to participate in influencer scouting through a simple guided workflow, reducing reliance on a large manually managed scout team.
manager.impact2Body: Improved first-reply conversion to 20% by introducing a more structured pre-message engagement flow before outreach.
manager.impact3Body: Shifted the process from training thousands of scouts to a more lightweight system supported by product guidance and AI follow-up.
manager.goalBody: The main goal was to turn influencer scouting into a scalable product workflow that could be handled not only by trained scouts, but also by everyday users. This required designing a clear and guided outreach experience, improving the quality of first contact through structured pre-message engagement, and reducing dependence on a large manually managed scout operation by shifting more of the process into product logic and AI-supported follow-up.
manager.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
manager.role1Body: Mapping complex core product logic and multi-sided marketplace flows.
manager.role2Body: Establishing a high-fidelity visual design language for the mobile-first era.
manager.role3Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
manager.role4Body: Developing complex interactive prototypes for rapid user validation.
manager.processIntro: Building the product from scratch, I translated an existing scout training guide into a simpler in-product experience.
manager.process1Body: The starting point was a manual instruction used to train scouts. I worked on simplifying that process and embedding key guidance directly into onboarding, reducing the need for manual training.
manager.process2Body: I first created the main product structure and flow, which then became the foundation for the interface design. I worked across the full journey, from registration to sending the first message.
manager.process3Body: To validate the experience, we ran A/B tests and collected feedback from scouts. This helped us identify friction points and refine the flow based on real usage.
manager.process4Body: One of the key iterations focused on the first message flow. After redesigning this step, we saw a positive impact on conversion.
manager.decisionsIntro: The core UX decisions focused on simplifying scouting for untrained users while improving the quality and effectiveness of influencer outreach.
manager.decision1Body: The product was built for people with little to no training. That meant the flow had to be extremely straightforward, with minimal decision-making and clear task progression.
manager.decision2Title: Turning outreach into a guided workflow
manager.decision2Body: Instead of asking users to figure out how to contact influencers on their own, the product gave them a structured sequence: find a creator, complete the required actions, copy the generated message, and send it through Instagram.
manager.decision3Title: Adding pre-message engagement to improve reply rates
manager.decision3Body: To unlock the outreach message, scouts had to follow the influencer, like a post, leave a comment, and only then send the welcome message. Although this added steps, it improved contact quality and helped increase first-response conversion to 20% — roughly on par with trained scouts.
manager.decision4Title: Using AI to take over after the first reply
manager.decision4Body: Once an influencer responded, the conversation no longer depended on the scout. An AI bot continued the communication and guided the influencer toward registration, reducing the skill required from users and making the system more scalable.
manager.impactIntro: Early outcomes from launch showed the product's potential to support influencer acquisition more independently and efficiently.
manager.impactItem1: Validated a scalable alternative to manual scouting.
manager.impactItem2: Enabled untrained users to achieve a 20% first-reply conversion rate.
manager.impactItem3: Reached performance comparable to trained scouts.
manager.impactItem4: Reduced operational complexity through a guided workflow and AI follow-up.
manager.impactItem5: Showed that registration conversion still needed further optimization.

brands.title: Rizzult for Brands
brands.overview: Rizzult for Brands is a B2B platform that helps brands launch and manage full-cycle influencer marketing campaigns. It allows companies to create campaign briefs, get matched with relevant influencers, and track campaign performance in one place.
brands.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
brands.impact1Body: Designed and launched Rizzult for Brands from scratch as a new full-cycle influencer marketing platform.
brands.impact2Body: Turned a complex service workflow into a clear and easy-to-use product experience for brands.
brands.impact3Body: Created a centralized platform for campaign launch, management, and future business growth.
brands.goalBody:
The business goal was to launch a new B2B direction and create a full-cycle influencer marketing solution for brands.

The product goal was to design a platform that felt simple on the surface while supporting a complex workflow behind the scenes. Brands needed to be able to create campaigns easily, understand what would happen next, and manage ongoing campaign activity without being overwhelmed by operational complexity.
brands.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
brands.role1Body: Shaping product logic, user flows, and information architecture for a complex B2B workflow.
brands.role2Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
brands.role3Body: Leading the design direction and collaborating closely with product, founder, developers, sales, and a junior designer.
brands.role4Body: Translating ideas into structured wireframes and interactive prototypes to validate and communicate solutions.
brands.processIntro: The project started with a clear business need: to create a new B2B product direction around influencer marketing services.
brands.process1Body: I analyzed the market and competitor landscape to understand how similar services were positioned and where product opportunities existed.
brands.process2Body: Analyzed customer needs using insights from sales calls and client requests to better understand brand expectations for this type of service.
brands.process3Body: I mapped the end-to-end workflow for campaign creation and management, then built the core user flows to define how the service could work as a product.
brands.process4Body: I designed prototypes to validate the product structure and finalized the interface design for implementation under a tight timeline. Because the timeline was limited, the process had to stay focused and efficient. The goal was to translate a complex service model into a clear and usable product experience without overcomplicating the interface.
brands.decisionsIntro: One of the main design challenges was simplifying a complex B2B workflow. Behind the interface, the service involved multiple moving parts: campaign setup, internal handoff, influencer sourcing, outreach, and campaign tracking. The product needed to make this process feel clear and manageable for brand-side users.
brands.decision1Title: Broke a complex workflow into clear steps
brands.decision1Body: To reduce cognitive load, I structured campaign creation into a step-by-step flow. This helped users focus on one decision at a time and made the process of launching a campaign feel more approachable.
brands.decision2Title: Designed a dashboard structure for campaign visibility
brands.decision2Body: I created a dashboard that gave brands a clearer view of campaign progress and management. The goal was to make the service more transparent and allow users to understand the current status of their campaigns without needing to rely on manual communication.
brands.decision3Body: The product had to feel easy to use for brands while still supporting a fairly complex backend workflow. I focused on keeping the experience simple at the interface level while preserving the necessary functionality for campaign creation and post-launch management. These decisions were based on business goals, internal research, team input, and an understanding of user expectations gathered through sales and client-facing conversations.
brands.impactIntro: The product was successfully launched as a new B2B tool within the Rizzult ecosystem.
brands.impactItem1: The company introduced a new productized direction for working with brands.
brands.impactItem2: Brands were able to complete key campaign-related tasks more easily.
brands.impactItem3: Onboarding and campaign setup became more structured and easier to understand.
brands.impactItem4: The platform created a more scalable foundation for future growth.
brands.impactItem5: The experience reduced friction in a service-heavy workflow by translating it into a clearer digital product.
